title: Disorders of the Liver
authors: Mohammad Nasser Kabbany & Karen F. Murray

blocks.text:
ทำไม neonatologist ต้องเข้าใจ Disorders of the Liver?
Neonatal liver disease คร่อมหลาย specialty แต่ neonatologist ต้องรู้ 6 ข้อนี้: (1) Jaundice ที่ onset < 24 hr หรือ persist > 14 days (term) / >21 days (preterm) = ALWAYS investigate — ไม่มี 'probably breast milk'; (2) Conjugated bili ≥15% ของ total = never normal → urgent workup; (3) Biliary atresia: Kasai ก่อนอายุ 2 เดือน = outcome ดีกว่า — ทุกวันที่ delay = bile duct destruction ไปเรื่อย ๆ; (4) GALD (gestational alloimmune liver disease): ส่ง IVIG ทันที แม้ยังไม่ diagnose ชัด — side effects minimal; (5) Tyrosinemia type 1: NTBC (nitisinone) = standard of care, อย่าพลาด — survival ↑ >85% vs <40% with diet alone; (6) PFIC1+2 = GGT ปกติ/ต่ำ; PFIC3 = GGT สูง — สำคัญมากในการ differentiate type ก่อน treatment.
เป้าหมายการเรียนรู้วันนี้
อธิบาย hepatocyte functions ที่สำคัญ: protein synthesis, bilirubin metabolism, bile acid formation, gluconeogenesis, urea cycle
ใช้ algorithm ประเมิน conjugated hyperbilirubinemia ตั้งแต่ initial labs → US → liver biopsy → intraoperative cholangiogram
วินิจฉัยและรักษา Biliary Atresia: presentation, triangular cord sign, HIDA scan, Kasai portoenterostomy, timing
จำ Alagille syndrome (JAG1/NOTCH2): 5 organ systems + ยา ursodiol + Kasai DDx
รู้ PFIC 1/2/3: gene, GGT pattern, treatment; bile acid synthesis defects: cholic acid rx
วินิจฉัย Alpha-1 Antitrypsin deficiency: Pi phenotyping, PiZZ, PAS+ diastase-resistant globules
รู้ PNALD: risk factors, prevention (MCT, fish oil lipids, early EN), UDCA 20–30 mg/kg/day
แยก galactosemia (E. coli sepsis + jaundice → สอย soy formula) จาก tyrosinemia (succinylacetone + NTBC) จาก HFI (aldolase B + fructose avoidance)
รู้ FAO defects: MCADD + LCHADD + CACT; urea cycle defects: OTC deficiency; lysosomal: Gaucher, NPC, Wolman
วินิจฉัยและรักษา GALD: ferritin >800 (มัก >15,000), AFP >300,000, C5b-9 on biopsy → IVIG + double-volume exchange transfusion
รู้ Infantile Hepatic Hemangioma (IHH): ≥5 cutaneous lesions → screen for visceral involvement; propranolol 1–3 mg/kg/day
รู้ hepatoblastoma risk factors + AFP interpretation ใน neonatal period
1. Liver Function & Lab Approach to Neonatal Liver Disease
1.1 Key Hepatocyte Functions
Protein synthesis: albumin, fibrinogen, prothrombin (hepatocyte only) — coagulopathy + hypoalbuminemia = significant hepatocyte loss
Bile: bile acids + bilirubin + cholesterol + phospholipids → secreted into canaliculus → ducts → intestine. Disruption = cholestasis
Bile สำคัญสำหรับ: solubilizing fat-soluble vitamins (A, D, E, K) → cholestasis = fat-soluble vitamin deficiency (bleeding, fractures, ↓ vision)
Portal hypertension: portal pressure >10 mmHg → splenomegaly + ascites + variceal bleeding; etiology: prehepatic / intrahepatic / posthepatic
1.2 Laboratory Assessment of Liver Disease

rows: Hepatocellular injury (not liver-specific — also in RBC, muscle)
Mild elevation common; not specific for liver alone
High in preterm with osteopenia — check GGT to clarify
PFIC1+2: GGT low/normal; PFIC3: GGT elevated
First-line imaging; gallbladder absence or triangular cord sign → BA

blocks.text:
กฎทอง: Conjugated Bili ≥15% = NEVER Normal
ไม่ว่า term หรือ preterm — ถ้า conjugated bilirubin ≥15% ของ total bilirubin = ต้องหา cause เสมอ. Physiologic jaundice และ breast milk jaundice ไม่ทำให้ conjugated bili สูง. Term infant: jaundice > 14 days → evaluate; preterm: > 21 days. อย่าพลาด biliary atresia — Kasai ก่อน 2 เดือน = outcome ดีกว่ามาก.
1.3 Algorithm: Approach to Conjugated Hyperbilirubinemia
Initial evaluation →
Step 1: Total + direct bili, CBC, ALT, AST, ALP, GGT, INR + blood culture (sepsis workup) + abdominal US
Step 2: If US shows choledochal cyst or mass → surgery. If normal or consistent with BA → proceed
Step 3: TSH, cortisol, A1AT phenotype, plasma amino acids, urine organic acids, urine succinylacetone, urine reducing substances, acylcarnitine, sweat chloride
Step 4: Liver biopsy — if consistent with obstructive cholangiopathy → HIDA scan (pretreat with phenobarbital 5 mg/kg/day × 5 days) → intraoperative cholangiogram
Step 5: Intraoperative cholangiogram confirms BA → immediate Kasai hepatic portoenterostomy
2. Biliary Atresia (BA)
2.1 Definition & Epidemiology
BA = idiopathic progressive inflammation + fibrosis → obliteration of extrahepatic biliary ducts within weeks of birth → biliary cirrhosis
Most common cause of infantile chronic liver disease + #1 pediatric liver transplant indication
Incidence: 0.5–3.2 per 10,000 live births; higher in Far East + Oceania
Two phenotypes: Sporadic/nonsyndromic 85–90% (no other anomalies) vs Embryonic/syndromic 10–15% (situs inversus, polysplenia/asplenia, cardiac, intestinal malrotation)
Without intervention: uniform fatality by 2 years of age
2.2 Clinical Presentation & Diagnosis
Presentation: 2–5 weeks of age; cholestatic jaundice + hepatosplenomegaly; acholic stools (often after jaundice onset); progressive weight gain failure
Labs: conjugated bili 2–7 mg/dL; total bili 5–12 mg/dL; ↑ ALT, ALP, GGT

US findings: absent/small gallbladder after fasting OR triangular cord sign (echogenic fibrous remnant at porta hepatis) — sensitivity ~80% (Lee Radiology 2003). Presence of gallbladder does NOT exclude BA.

HIDA scan: rapid tracer uptake but no excretion into bowel at 24 hr; pretreat with phenobarbital 5 mg/kg/day × 5 days ↑ sensitivity. Caution: functional cholestasis (hypothyroidism) also gives non-excreting scan
Liver biopsy: bile ductular proliferation + bile duct plugging; MMP-7 (matrix metalloproteinase-7) has good accuracy but needs more validation (Yang Hepatology 2018)
Confirmation: intraoperative cholangiogram (atretic biliary tree + no patency)
2.3 Treatment — Kasai Portoenterostomy
Kasai = hepatic portoenterostomy: excise obstructed bile ducts → Roux-en-Y jejunal limb anastomosed to liver at porta hepatis → restores bile flow
Timing critical: Kasai before 2 months = significantly improved surgical success + outcome (Ohi Liver 2001; Shneider J Pediatr 2006)
Despite successful Kasai: most progress to cirrhosis + portal hypertension → eventually require liver transplantation

⚠️ Biliary Atresia: ทุกวันที่ delay = bile duct ถูกทำลายไปเรื่อย ๆ
Rule: ถ้าสงสัย BA → refer pediatric hepatologist ทันที. Kasai ก่อน 2 เดือน = dramatically better outcome. Acholic stools ใน infant = medical emergency จนกว่าจะพิสูจน์ไม่ใช่ BA. ประเทศไทยมี BA registry — ให้ส่งต่อ KCMH ผ่าน hepatology. อย่ารอให้ HIDA scan result ก่อน refer — HIDA scan ไม่ควร delay definitive evaluation.
3. Structural & Syndromic Cholestatic Disorders
3.1 Alagille Syndrome (ALGS)
Autosomal dominant / de novo; prevalence 1:30,000; >90% carry JAG1 mutation (chr 20) — Notch signaling pathway ligand; small number: NOTCH2
Multisystem syndrome — จำ 5 organs + renal:
(1) Cholestatic liver disease: bile duct paucity on biopsy — bile duct:portal tract ratio <0.9; elevated GGT + bili; intractable pruritus; xanthomas
(2) Characteristic facies: prominent forehead + pointed chin (triangular face) + deep-set eyes + hypertelorism + saddle nose — often subtle in neonatal period
(3) Congenital heart disease: peripheral pulmonary stenosis most common; other CHD possible
(4) Ocular: posterior embryotoxon (prominence of Schwalbe line) — slit lamp exam; most common
(5) Skeletal: butterfly vertebrae + rib abnormalities
(6) Renal: renal disease in variable forms
Metabolic bone disease striking in ALGS (ALP ↑↑ reflects bone + biliary); xanthomas from hypercholesterolemia; 21–31% eventually require liver transplant
Biopsy pitfall: bile duct paucity is normal in preterm — careful not to overdiagnose ALGS in premature infants
DDx from BA: ALGS GGT ↑↑ + syndromic features + JAG1 testing; BA has ductular proliferation NOT paucity
3.2 Choledochal Cysts
Congenital dilations of biliary ducts; higher incidence in Asian population, female predominance; 80% diagnosed in first decade
5 types: Type I (most common) = spherical/fusiform dilation of extrahepatic biliary tree; Type IV = multiple cysts (intra + extrahepatic)
Pathogenesis: anomalous pancreaticobiliary union → pancreatic secretion reflux into bile duct
Presentation in infants: cholestasis (most common); older patients: cholangitis/pancreatitis; diagnosis by US
Treatment: complete surgical excision; long-term surveillance for cholangiocarcinoma + squamous cell carcinoma; associated with cholangitis + cirrhosis risk
3.3 Congenital Hepatic Fibrosis (CHF) & Caroli Syndrome
CHF = hereditary malformation of bile ducts from failed embryonic remodeling; autosomal recessive; associated with ARPKD, Joubert syndrome, Bardet-Biedl, Ivemark, Caroli
Caroli syndrome: CHF + cystic dilation of intrahepatic ducts; present as portal hypertension + splenomegaly; ↑ cholangitis risk
Characteristic: normal transaminases + intact synthetic function despite advancing portal hypertension — ต่างจาก other chronic liver disease
Diagnosis: US + Doppler or MRI/MRCP; if with ARPKD can diagnose clinically; if isolated → liver biopsy
Treatment: manage portal hypertension complications + prevent cholangitis; Caroli = focal → hepatic lobectomy; diffuse → liver transplant
4. Metabolic & Genetic Causes of Cholestasis
4.1 Alpha-1 Antitrypsin (A1AT) Deficiency
Most common genetic cause of liver disease in children; affects ~1:2,000 live births; autosomal codominant
Mechanism: single amino acid substitution → abnormal A1AT folding → trapped in Golgi → hepatocellular injury from accumulation
Normal variant = M; disease variants: Z (slowest migration, most severe) and S; PiZZ = worst; PiSZ and PiMZ also cause liver disease
Presentation: cholestatic jaundice in 10% of PiZZ infants; hepatosplenomegaly; pulmonary disease manifests in adulthood
Diagnosis: A1AT Pi phenotyping (Pi type) — NOT serum level (serum level falsely normal in inflammation/infection); biopsy: PAS+ diastase-resistant eosinophilic globules in hepatocytes

Treatment: supportive; MCT formula + fat-soluble vitamin supplementation; ursodiol (no proven benefit but used); liver transplant → recipient assumes donor Pi phenotype → normal function; avoid smoking
4.2 Progressive Familial Intrahepatic Cholestasis (PFIC)

rows: Extrahepatic: deafness, diarrhea, pancreatitis, short stature; intractable pruritus
More rapidly progressive than PFIC1; risk of hepatocellular carcinoma (even post-transplant)
Phospholipid deficiency → unstable micelles → intrahepatic cholangiopathy; presents later; cholesterol gallstones

blocks.text: All PFIC: markedly elevated serum bile acids, mildly elevated bilirubin; GGT normal/low = PFIC1+2; GGT elevated = PFIC3
Treatment: MCT formula + fat-soluble vitamins + ursodiol + antihistamines + cholestyramine + rifampin; refractory: partial biliary diversion / ileal exclusion / liver transplant
4.3 Disorders of Bile Acid Synthesis
Rare AR disorders (prevalence ~1:50,000); accumulation of hepatotoxic intermediate bile acid metabolites
Key biochemical marker: normal or LOW serum bile acids + elevated aminotransferases + NORMAL GGT (GGT ปกติเป็น clue สำคัญ!) + fat-soluble vitamin deficiency
If serum bile acids low → measure urinary bile acids to identify specific defect
Treatment: cholic acid suppresses toxic intermediate production (Ursodiol NOT indicated — ไม่ suppress toxic intermediates)

PFIC Biochem Mnemonic: GGT ต่ำ = Type 1+2; GGT สูง = Type 3
PFIC1 + PFIC2: GGT normal/low — both have defective bile export. PFIC3: GGT elevated — phospholipid deficiency. Bile acid synthesis defects: GGT normal + serum bile acids LOW (paradox — no bile acids formed). การรู้ GGT pattern ช่วย narrow diagnosis ก่อน genetic testing.
5. Parenteral Nutrition-Associated Liver Disease (PNALD)
Incidence: 33–85% of premature infants receiving TPN > 7 days develop PNALD (Koseesirikul Pediatr Int 2012)
Primary phenotype: cholestasis; short duration TPN (<2 weeks) → usually resolves; prolonged TPN → irreversible liver failure
Lab definition: conjugated bili >2 mg/dL + ALT >2× ULN in infant receiving TPN
Risk factors: prematurity + low birth weight + lack of enteral feeding + sepsis + short gut + NEC
Management:
Early EN: most important intervention — even trophic feeds help stimulate bile flow
Lipid minimization: <1 g/kg/day when cholestasis develops
Omega-3 / fish oil-based lipid (SMOF) vs soy-based → more effective at resolving cholestasis
MCT formula: absorbed directly into portal system without bile acid emulsification
Ursodiol (UDCA) 20–30 mg/kg/day divided doses → stimulates bile flow
Prevent infection: sepsis is major risk factor for progression
6. Metabolic Liver Disease
6.1 Disorders of Carbohydrate Metabolism
Galactosemia (Classic)
Deficiency: galactose-1-phosphate uridyl transferase (Gal-1-PUT); AR; incidence 1:60,000; toxic metabolites accumulate in liver, brain, kidney, eye lens
Classic presentation: failure to thrive + jaundice + vomiting + diarrhea after breast milk or milk-based formula (lactose); E. coli sepsis + jaundice + coagulopathy = classic NICU presentation
Gold standard diagnosis: quantitative RBC Gal-1-PUT activity; NBS may identify; positive urine reducing substances while ingesting galactose

Treatment: immediate dietary galactose restriction + soy-based (nongalactose) formula + IV glucose + Vitamin K + antibiotics; neurodevelopmental follow-up even with treatment; ophthalmology referral (lens)

E. coli Sepsis + Jaundice + Coagulopathy ใน Newborn = Think Galactosemia
Classic triad ที่พบบ่อยใน NICU: E. coli sepsis + jaundice + coagulopathy + vomiting ใน newborn ที่ยังดื่มนม lactose-containing. ส่ง NBS + urine reducing substances + RBC Gal-1-PUT ทันที. Switch to soy formula ก่อน result. Untreated galactosemia มี E. coli susceptibility เพราะ galactose-1-phosphate accumulation impairs neutrophil function.
Glycogen Storage Diseases (GSD)

GSD Type I (von Gierke): AR; deficient glucose-6-phosphatase → hepatomegaly + fasting hypoglycemia (after sleep >4 hr) + lactic acidosis + hyperuricemia + hyperlipidemia + neutropenia (Ib); Ia = G6PC (80%); Ib = G6PT1 (<20%); treatment: continuous enteral feeds + cornstarch (slowly degraded)

GSD Type III (Cori/Forbes): debranching enzyme deficiency; milder hypoglycemia than GSD I; IIIa = liver + muscle (cardiomyopathy later); IIIb = liver only; treatment mirrors GSD I + protein can be gluconeogenic source
GSD Type IV (Andersen): GBE1 mutation; branching enzyme deficiency; may present prenatally with hydrops; postnatal: hypotonia + cardiomyopathy; liver transplant for ESLD
6.2 Disorders of Amino Acid Metabolism
Tyrosinemia Type 1
Deficiency: fumarylacetoacetate hydrolase (FAH); AR; incidence 1:100,000; presents as fulminant neonatal liver failure or chronic hepatitis
Diagnosis: blood/urine succinylacetone (most sensitive specific marker); molecular FAH testing to confirm; serum tyrosine NOT sensitive/specific

Treatment: NTBC (nitisinone/2-(2-nitro-4-trifluromethylbenzoyl)-1,3-cyclohexanedione) → inhibits 4-hydroxyphenylpyruvate dioxygenase → prevents succinylacetone formation. Survival: >85% at 1 yr with NTBC vs <40% with diet alone (standard of care). Diet: restrict tyrosine + phenylalanine formula. Long-term HCC risk → surveillance AFP + US
Maple Syrup Urine Disease (MSUD)
Branched-chain α-ketoacid dehydrogenase complex deficiency; AR; NBS in US; accumulation of valine + leucine + isoleucine + toxic ketoacids
Presentation: vomiting + acidosis + sweet maple syrup urine odor + neurocognitive dysfunction/AMS; mildly elevated transaminases; hypoglycemia; hyperammonemia
Treatment: dietary restriction branched-chain AAs + specialized formula + avoid catabolism; dialysis for severe crisis; liver transplant in refractory cases
6.3 Fatty Acid Oxidation (FAO) Defects

rows: Recurrent lethargy, emesis, coma from hypoketotic hypoglycemia; hepatitis; hyperammonemia
Fibroblast enzyme / genetic; NBS; avoid fasting; risk sudden death
Acute poor feeding + lethargy + hypotonia + hypoketotic hypoglycemia in neonatal period; later: neuropathy + retinopathy; maternal HELLP risk
NBS + fibroblast LCHADD activity; low-fat + MCT diet; high morbidity
Hypoketotic hypoglycemia + hepatic dysfunction + cardiomyopathy + seizures; hyperammonemia; low carnitine; abnormal acyl carnitine
Genetic testing; low-fat + MCT + carnitine; high mortality with cardiomyopathy
Hepatomegaly + encephalopathy/coma in fasting/illness; normal AST; no urine ketones; normal acyl carnitine
Absence urinary ketones + molecular testing; avoid fasting

blocks.text: 6.4 Urea Cycle Defects
Most common: OTC (ornithine transcarbamylase) deficiency (X-linked); carbamoyl phosphate synthase deficiency; citrullinemia — all autosomal recessive except OTC
Present: first days of life with hyperammonemia + cerebral edema; mimics sepsis
Diagnosis: hyperammonemia + low BUN + abnormal serum amino acid pattern; confirm by molecular genetic testing
Treatment: metabolic specialist; maintain serum ammonia normal; prevent catabolism; protein-restricted formula (½ essential AAs); dialysis for severe crisis; liver transplant for refractory
6.5 Lysosomal Storage Disorders

Gaucher disease: AR; glucocerebrosidase deficiency → glycolipid accumulation. Incidence 1:75,000; higher in Ashkenazi Jewish. Presentation: hepatosplenomegaly + normal to mildly elevated liver enzymes. Diagnosis: leukocyte glucocerebrosidase activity + biopsy (Gaucher cells = lipid-filled macrophages). Treatment: enzyme replacement therapy (ERT)

Niemann-Pick Disease Type C (NPC): NPC1/NPC2 mutations; impaired LDL cholesterol transport → hepatosplenomegaly + progressive neurocognitive degeneration (dystonia, seizures, ataxia); ↓ HDL, ↑ TG, ↑ LDL; diagnosis: fibroblast culture + genetic; treatment: largely supportive

Lysosomal acid lipase deficiency — Wolman disease (severe): failure to thrive + steatorrhea + hepatosplenomegaly + jaundice; adrenal calcifications on imaging (pathognomonic); death in year 1; ERT trials promising. CESD = milder late-onset form
6.6 Mitochondrial Hepatopathies
Respiratory chain complex deficiencies → acute liver failure in neonatal period; suspect if: week 1 life + hypoglycemia + hypotonia + seizures + evolving hepatic synthetic dysfunction
Diagnosis: elevated lactate; lactate:pyruvate ratio > 30 = strongly suggestive; mitochondrial DNA mutational analysis
Treatment: few targeted therapies; liver transplant if no additional comorbidities limiting post-transplant survival
7. Gestational Alloimmune Liver Disease (GALD)
7.1 Pathogenesis — บทเรียนสำคัญ
GALD เดิมเรียก neonatal hemochromatosis แต่ iron overload = phenotype, ไม่ใช่ primary cause. Pathogenesis ที่แท้จริง:
Maternal IgG (ข้ามรก freely) → anti-fetal hepatocyte IgG → complement-mediated hepatocyte destruction → liver volume reduced 10–25%

Reduced fetal liver → ↓ hepcidin → ↓ regulation of placental iron delivery → iron overload in extrahepatic tissues: pancreas, salivary glands, heart, thyroid (RES spared: spleen, bone marrow, lymph nodes = ต่างจาก hereditary hemochromatosis)
7.2 Clinical Presentation & Diagnosis
Typically sick at birth; fetal: oligohydramnios / IUGR. Neonatal: acute liver failure + hypoglycemia + coagulopathy + extreme coagulopathy; cholestasis evolves over time, NOT present at birth
Labs: normal to slightly ↑ ALT; serum ferritin typically >800 ng/mL (commonly >15,000 ng/mL); AFP > 80,000 ng/mL (often >300,000 ng/mL); iron saturation >90%
Liver biopsy: reduced hepatocyte volume + C5b-9 (complement membrane attack complex) on immunohistochemistry + hepatocyte collapse + fibrosis + siderosis
Confirm extrahepatic siderosis: mucosal biopsy (submucosal glandular biopsy) → if negative + still suspected → T2-weighted MRI (pancreas, thyroid, myocardium, salivary glands)
7.3 Treatment
Acute: IVIG 1 g/kg IMMEDIATELY — even before workup complete (side effects minimal, no harm if alternate diagnosis). Confirmed GALD → double-volume exchange transfusion + second dose IVIG
Old treatments (iron chelation, antioxidants): no role in current practice
Outcome with IVIG + exchange transfusion: survival improved to 75–80% without liver transplant (Rand J Pediatr 2009)
If transplant needed: complicated by infant size + multiorgan failure; graft loss + death rates high but comparable to other neonatal ALF indications
7.4 Prevention in Subsequent Pregnancies
Recurrence risk: >90% probability of GALD in subsequent infant born to mother whose previous child had GALD
Prevention: IVIG infusions during pregnancy starting at 14 weeks gestational age, then 16 weeks, then weekly from 18 weeks until delivery (Whitington Lancet 2004; evidence evolving)

GALD: ส่ง IVIG ทันที ไม่ต้องรอผล
ถ้าสงสัย GALD ให้ IVIG 1 g/kg ทันที — side effects minimal. อย่ารอ workup ให้ complete. ถ้า confirm: double-volume exchange transfusion + IVIG ซ้ำ. Flag ที่ต้องรู้: ferritin >800 + AFP >300,000 + coagulopathy + hypoglycemia ใน neonate = GALD จนกว่าจะพิสูจน์ไม่ใช่. แม่ที่เคยมี affected child: IVIG ตั้งแต่ 14 wks GA ทุกครรภ์ต่อไป.
8. Vascular Malformations & Liver Tumors
8.1 Infantile Hepatic Hemangiomas (IHH)
Most common benign tumor of infancy; 1–2% of newborns; up to 10% of infants by 1 year
3 types: Focal (usually incidental, asymptomatic, no cutaneous lesion); Multifocal (multiple + cutaneous hemangiomas; presents in first weeks); Diffuse (hepatomegaly + heart failure + anemia)

Clinical significance: ≥5 cutaneous hemangiomas → screen for visceral (hepatic, brain, lung) involvement; multifocal: heart failure + anemia + thrombocytopenia; hemangioma can inactivate thyroxine → hypothyroidism; rare: intraperitoneal hemorrhage
Natural history: proliferative phase birth–12 months → involution 2–10 years

Treatment: asymptomatic = observe. Symptomatic: propranolol 1–3 mg/kg/day until proliferation phase ~12 months (Léauté-Labrèze NEJM 2008). Second-line: steroids, vincristine, alfa-2a interferon; embolization/resection if medical fails
8.2 Congenital Portosystemic Shunts
Allow intestinal venous blood to bypass liver: intrahepatic (patent ductus venosus) or extrahepatic (Abernathy malformation)
Manifestations: elevated serum ammonia if significant portal diversion; NOT typically causing portal hypertension
Many close spontaneously; those causing encephalopathy / cardiopulmonary compromise → embolization / surgical ligation / liver transplant
8.3 Hepatoblastoma
Most common malignant liver tumor in neonates / infants; risk factors: prematurity, low birth weight, FAP, Beckwith-Wiedemann, Li-Fraumeni, trisomy 18, GSD Ia
Presentation: abdominal mass + weight loss; AFP ↑ (normal term infant AFP can be up to 100,000 ng/mL → interpret with trend + age)
Diagnosis: MRI preferred; biopsy for epithelial subtype; AFP is prognostic marker after therapy
Treatment: resection ± chemotherapy; liver transplant if unresectable
9. NICU Practical — Jaundiced Infant ที่ NICU
New/persistent jaundice ≥14 days term (≥21 days preterm): ส่ง total + direct bili ทันที — ถ้า conjugated ≥15% → urgent workup; อย่า assume breast milk jaundice
Acholic stools: medical emergency จนกว่าจะ exclude BA. ส่ง conjugated bili + US ทันที + refer hepatology. อย่าให้ HIDA scan delay surgical referral.
Galactosemia: E. coli sepsis + jaundice + coagulopathy ใน newborn = ส่ง RBC Gal-1-PUT, switch to soy formula ทันที ก่อน result
Tyrosinemia: liver failure + ↑ succinylacetone = NTBC ทันที + dietary restriction; อย่ารอ FAH confirmation
GALD: sick neonate + extreme coagulopathy + ferritin >800 + AFP >300,000 = IVIG 1 g/kg ทันที แม้ยังไม่ diagnose
PNALD prevention: เริ่ม trophic feeds โดยเร็ว แม้แต่ 0.5–1 mL/hr; lipids <1 g/kg/day ถ้า cholestasis; พิจารณา SMOFlipid (fish oil-based)
IHH workup: ≥5 skin hemangiomas → US liver + check TFT (IHH อาจ inactivate T4 → acquired hypothyroidism)
A1AT workup: ส่ง Pi phenotype (NOT serum level); ถ้า PiZZ → MCT formula + fat-soluble vitamins + GI hepatology consult; avoid environmental pollution
PFIC diagnosis: GGT ต่ำ + markedly elevated serum bile acids + intractable pruritus = PFIC1 or 2 → genetic panel ABCB11/ATP8B1
Urea cycle / organic acid crisis: hyperammonemia + altered mental status + low BUN ใน neonate = urea cycle defect จนกว่าจะพิสูจน์ไม่ใช่ → metabolic specialist + ammonia scavengers + stop protein
10. 5 Bedside Pearls — สำหรับพรุ่งนี้

PEARL 1 — Acholic Stools = Emergency ไม่ใช่ Constipation
Acholic (pale/white/clay) stools ใน infant = biliary obstruction จนกว่าจะพิสูจน์ไม่ใช่. Biliary atresia ต้อง Kasai ก่อนอายุ 2 เดือน — ทุกสัปดาห์ที่ delay ↑ fibrosis. ส่ง US + refer hepatology วันเดียวกัน. อย่าส่ง HIDA scan ก่อน — อย่าให้ delay refer.

PEARL 2 — E. coli Sepsis + Jaundice + Coagulopathy = Galactosemia
Newborn ที่ดื่มนมแล้วมี E. coli sepsis + jaundice + coagulopathy = galactosemia จนกว่าจะ prove otherwise. Switch to soy formula ทันที ก่อน result. Galactose-1-phosphate ทำให้ neutrophil function บกพร่อง → susceptible to gram-negative sepsis. ส่ง RBC Gal-1-PUT assay — อย่าอ่าน NBS เดี่ยว ๆ หลัง RBC transfusion (unreliable).

PEARL 3 — GALD: ให้ IVIG ก่อน Workup Complete
Sick neonate + coagulopathy extreme + ferritin >800 + AFP >300,000 + hypoglycemia = GALD. ให้ IVIG 1 g/kg ทันที — side effects ต่ำมาก, ไม่ทำให้ alternate diagnosis worse. Confirm → double-volume exchange transfusion + IVIG ครั้งที่ 2. แม่ที่เคยมี GALD child: IVIG ทุกสัปดาห์ตั้งแต่ 18 wk GA ใน next pregnancy.

PEARL 4 — PFIC: GGT คือ Key ในการ Classify
Conjugated hyperbilirubinemia + intractable pruritus + markedly elevated serum bile acids: PFIC1+2 = GGT normal/low; PFIC3 = GGT elevated. Bile acid synthesis defects = GGT normal + serum bile acids LOW (paradox). GGT ปกติ ≠ ไม่มี cholestasis — ต้องดู serum bile acids ด้วย. Ursodiol ใช้ใน PFIC แต่ไม่ใช่ bile acid synthesis defects.

PEARL 5 — ≥5 Cutaneous Hemangiomas → Screen IHH + TFT
Rule ง่าย ๆ: hemangioma บนผิวหนัง ≥5 ตำแหน่ง = ต้อง screen visceral involvement (US liver, lung, brain). IHH multifocal อาจ inactivate T4 → acquired hypothyroidism — ส่ง free T4 + TSH. Asymptomatic = observe; symptomatic = propranolol 1–3 mg/kg/day (Léauté-Labrèze NEJM 2008).
11. Evidence Update — Landmark Studies

Fawaz R et al. J Pediatr Gastroenterol Nutr 2017;64(1):154: NASPGHAN/ESPGHAN guideline for evaluation of cholestatic jaundice in infants — defines conjugated bili threshold ≥15%, workup timing (>14 days term, >21 days preterm), algorithm for BA evaluation.
Ohi R. Liver 2001;21:175: Kasai portoenterostomy for BA — surgical technique and importance of timing; early Kasai (<2 months) associated with improved bile flow and survival.
Shneider BL et al. J Pediatr 2006;148:467 (BASM): North American multicenter study of BA outcomes 1997–2000; factors associated with clearance of jaundice post-Kasai; majority progress to transplant.

Schwarz KB et al. Hepatology 2013;58(5):1724: Large North American multicenter BA study — extrahepatic anomalies in infants with BA; embryonic syndromic form with laterality defects has distinct subgroups.
Yang L et al. Hepatology 2018;68(6):2069: MMP-7 serum marker for BA diagnosis — good diagnostic accuracy; potential non-invasive marker but needs further validation.
Lee H-J et al. Radiology 2003;229:395: Triangular cord sign on US for BA diagnosis — sensitivity ~80%; objective criteria for echogenic cord at porta hepatis.
Rand EB et al. J Pediatr 2009;155:566: IVIG + double-volume exchange transfusion for GALD — survival improved to 75–80% vs historical mortality with older medical therapies.
Whitington PF, Hibbard JU. Lancet 2004;364:1690: High-dose IVIG during pregnancy for recurrent neonatal hemochromatosis (GALD) — foundation for current prevention protocol from 14 weeks GA.
Taylor SA et al. J Pediatr 2018;196:123: Effects of GALD on fetal and infant morbidity/mortality; >90% recurrence risk in subsequent pregnancies without IVIG prophylaxis.
Chinsky JM et al. Genet Med 2017;19(12): US/Canadian consensus for tyrosinemia type I — NTBC as standard of care; dietary restrictions; AFP + US surveillance for HCC long-term.
Holme E, Lindstedt S. J Inherit Metab Dis 1998;21:507: Landmark NTBC (nitisinone) trial — survival improved to >85% at 1 year from <40% with diet alone in tyrosinemia type 1.

Léauté-Labrèze C et al. NEJM 2008;359:2649: Propranolol for severe hemangiomas of infancy — landmark trial establishing propranolol as first-line treatment; mechanism: vasoconstriction + apoptosis of hemangioma cells.
Lane E, Murray KF. Pediatr Clin NA 2021;64(3):621: Comprehensive review of neonatal cholestasis — diagnostic approach, DDx, management of all cholestatic etiologies in the neonatal period.
Koseesirikul P et al. Pediatr Int 2012;54:434: Thai study: incidence and risk factors of PNALD in newborn infants — 33–85% of premature infants with TPN >7 days develop PNALD.
Mitchell E et al. Clin Liver Dis 2018;22(4):625: Alagille syndrome review — JAG1/NOTCH2 mutations, multisystem involvement, treatment including 21–31% requiring liver transplantation.
Strnad P et al. NEJM 2020;382(15):1443: Alpha-1-antitrypsin deficiency comprehensive review — molecular mechanism of Z protein accumulation, liver and lung disease, liver transplant outcomes.
12. Self-Check Questions พร้อมเฉลย
Q: Define conjugated hyperbilirubinemia. Significance คืออะไร? workup เมื่อไหร่ใน term vs preterm?

ตอบ: Conjugated hyperbilirubinemia = conjugated bilirubin ≥15% ของ total bilirubin = NEVER normal — always warrants investigation. Term infant: jaundice persisting > 14 days; preterm: > 21 days → evaluate urgently. Initial workup: total + direct bili + CBC + ALT + AST + ALP + GGT + INR + abdominal US. Fawaz NASPGHAN/ESPGHAN guideline 2017 เป็น reference หลัก.
Q: Biliary atresia: typical presentation, key imaging findings, surgical treatment, timing?

ตอบ: Present: 2–5 weeks of age; cholestatic jaundice + hepatosplenomegaly + acholic stools + poor weight gain. Imaging: US shows absent/small gallbladder after fasting or triangular cord sign (sensitivity 80%). HIDA: rapid uptake but no bowel excretion at 24 hr. Confirm: intraoperative cholangiogram. Treatment: Kasai hepatic portoenterostomy — Roux-en-Y jejunal loop to porta hepatis. Timing: before 2 months = significantly better outcome (Ohi 2001; Shneider 2006). Despite Kasai, most eventually require transplant.
Q: Alagille syndrome: gene, inheritance, 5 system involvement, ข้อ biopsy pitfall?

ตอบ: Gene: JAG1 (>90%) on chromosome 20, Notch signaling ligand; small number NOTCH2; autosomal dominant or de novo. 5 systems: (1) Cholestatic liver disease (bile duct paucity); (2) Characteristic facies (triangular: prominent forehead + pointed chin); (3) CHD (peripheral pulmonary stenosis most common); (4) Ocular: posterior embryotoxon; (5) Skeletal: butterfly vertebrae; + renal disease. Biopsy pitfall: bile duct paucity = NORMAL in premature infants — do not diagnose pathologic paucity in preterm.
Q: PFIC types: gene, GGT pattern, clinical differentiator ที่สำคัญ?

ตอบ: PFIC1 (Byler, ATP8B1): GGT normal/low + extrahepatic features (deafness, diarrhea, pancreatitis). PFIC2 (ABCB11/BSEP): GGT normal/low + most rapidly progressive + ↑ HCC risk even post-transplant. PFIC3 (ABCB4/MDR3): GGT ELEVATED + phospholipid deficiency + cholesterol gallstones; presents later. All: markedly elevated serum bile acids + intractable pruritus. Treatment: ursodiol / biliary diversion / transplant.
Q: GALD: pathogenesis, diagnosis, treatment protocol, prevention?

ตอบ: Pathogenesis: maternal IgG → anti-fetal hepatocyte antibody → complement-mediated (C5b-9) hepatocyte destruction → ↓ hepcidin → iron overload in extrahepatic tissues (pancreas, thyroid, salivary gland, myocardium — spleen/bone marrow spared). Diagnosis: ferritin >800 (often >15,000) + AFP >300,000 + iron sat >90% + liver biopsy (C5b-9 on immunohisto) + extrahepatic siderosis (biopsy or T2-MRI). Treatment: IVIG 1 g/kg immediately → double-volume exchange transfusion → second IVIG. Survival 75–80% (Rand 2009). Prevention: IVIG starting 14 wk GA weekly from 18 wk in subsequent pregnancies (Whitington Lancet 2004).
Q: Tyrosinemia type 1: diagnosis marker ที่ specific สุด? treatment? ยา NTBC mechanism?

ตอบ: Most specific diagnostic marker: succinylacetone in blood or urine (NBS in many US states). Serum tyrosine = NOT sensitive/specific. Confirm: FAH molecular testing. Treatment: NTBC (nitisinone) + dietary tyrosine/phenylalanine restriction. NTBC mechanism: inhibits 4-hydroxyphenylpyruvate dioxygenase → prevents formation of maleylacetoacetic acid and fumarylacetoacetic acid (precursors to hepatotoxic succinylacetone). Survival: >85% at 1 yr with NTBC vs <40% diet alone (Holme 1998). Long-term: HCC surveillance (AFP + US).
Q: Alpha-1 antitrypsin deficiency: ทำไม serum level ไม่ควรใช้ diagnose? test ที่ถูกต้อง? histology?

ตอบ: Serum A1AT level falsely elevated during inflammation or infection (acute phase reactant) → can appear normal even in deficiency state. Correct test: A1AT Pi phenotyping (Pi type); identifies M, S, Z variants by electrophoretic migration. PiZZ = most severe; PiSZ, PiMZ also cause liver disease. Histology: PAS+ diastase-resistant eosinophilic globules in hepatocytes = trapped abnormal A1AT protein in endoplasmic reticulum (not pathognomonic; also seen bile duct destruction → important to exclude BA and ALGS).
Q: PNALD: definition, risk factors, prevention strategies ที่ evidence-based?

ตอบ: PNALD definition: conjugated bili >2 mg/dL + ALT >2× ULN in infant receiving TPN. Incidence: 33–85% of premature infants with TPN >7 days. Risk factors: prematurity + low birth weight + no enteral feeding + sepsis + short gut + NEC. Prevention: (1) early enteral nutrition (even trophic); (2) lipid minimization <1 g/kg/day; (3) fish oil-based lipids (SMOFlipid) over soy-based; (4) UDCA 20–30 mg/kg/day; (5) prevent/treat sepsis aggressively.
Q: Infantile hepatic hemangioma: เมื่อไหร่ต้องกังวล? acquired hypothyroidism mechanism? treatment?

ตอบ: Flag: ≥5 cutaneous hemangiomas → screen for visceral IHH (US liver). Multifocal/diffuse IHH → heart failure + anemia + thrombocytopenia. Hypothyroidism mechanism: IHH cells express high levels of type 3 deiodinase → inactivate T4 → consumption hypothyroidism (ต่างจาก central/primary hypothyroidism). Check free T4 + TSH ใน IHH. Treatment: asymptomatic = observe; symptomatic = propranolol 1–3 mg/kg/day (NEJM 2008); second-line: steroids/vincristine/interferon.
Q: A neonate presents with hyperammonemia, altered mental status, and low BUN. Differential? immediate steps?

ตอบ: Low BUN + hyperammonemia + AMS ใน neonate = urea cycle defect until proven otherwise. DDx: OTC deficiency (most common, X-linked), CPS deficiency, citrullinemia, argininosuccinic aciduria. Immediate steps: (1) stop protein intake; (2) IV glucose + lipid to prevent catabolism; (3) ammonia scavengers (sodium benzoate + sodium phenylacetate); (4) arginine supplementation; (5) dialysis if refractory. Diagnosis: serum amino acid profile + urine organic acids + molecular testing. Metabolic specialist consult ทันที.
Day 66 Preview

Avery Chapter 66 — Developmental Hematology
พรุ่งนี้: Developmental Hematology — หลังเรียน disorders of the liver วันนี้ (รวม GALD ที่เชื่อมกับ hemostasis), เราจะมาเรียน hematopoiesis development ตั้งแต่ yolk sac → fetal liver → bone marrow: เนื้อหาหลัก: fetal Hb (HbF) + oxygen affinity, normal RBC values ตาม gestational age, iron metabolism in the newborn, physiologic anemia ของทารก vs pathologic anemia, และ developmental changes ใน leukocyte + platelet lineages — เชื่อมกับ Ch67 Bleeding/Thrombotic + Ch68 Platelet disorders ที่จะตามมา.
